Professor View 01 / 40
Travel English -- 40 Aulas

Pricila Adamo

De "eu sei o que quero dizer mas não consigo dizer" para "passaporte pronto, inglês pronto" -- falar com alegria, não com esforço, em 40 aulas

B1+ (B2 operacional) Araras, SP Dentista em transição para aposentadoria 60 min / Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Pricila Adamo
50 anos
Dentista em transição para aposentadoria
Araras, SP (trânsito frequente em São Paulo)
B1+ (B2 operacional)
Fluência conversacional para viagens e vida internacional
40 aulas de 60 min (3x/semana)
Online (Zoom)

Jornada de Transformação

De: "Falo inglês mas travo na hora de detalhar — sei o que quero dizer, mas na hora de organizar as coisas, fica pior. Fico cansada de estudar e quero só ter alegria."

Para: "Estou sempre pronta — se aparecer uma viagem, se aparecer um estrangeiro, se aparecer um filme sem legenda. O inglês virou parte da minha rotina, não uma obrigação." em 40 aulas

Promessa do Programa

"Eu gosto de estar aqui em passaporte, com tudo pronto." — De 'eu sei o que quero dizer mas não consigo dizer' para 'passaporte pronto, inglês pronto' — falar com alegria, não com esforço, em 40 aulas.

Forças

  • Ausência de vergonha comunicativa — fala sem filtro, mesmo errando
  • Iniciativa de imersão autônoma — cria micro-hábitos em inglês por conta própria
  • Alto engajamento temático — conecta o inglês a interesses genuínos
  • Autoconsciência linguística — sabe identificar seus gaps com precisão
  • Experiência real com o idioma — viagens internacionais, atendimento de pacientes anglófonos
  • Energia emocional estável e animada

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Densidade informacional em inglês — respostas curtas, perde detalhes
  • Estrutura sintática — frases incompletas, autocorreção frequente
  • Vocabulário ativo limitado — compreende mais do que produz
  • Compreensão auditiva de sotaques variados
  • Consistência na rotina de estudos — histórico de ciclos de abandono
  • Fadiga cognitiva ao estudar inglês

Currículo -- 40 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework
1Who Is Pricila Adamo? -- Diagnostic + Personal StoryPresent simple, past simple, present perfect (diagnostic)Personal timeline + diagnostic conversationRecord 3-min audio telling your story
2My Life in Chapters -- Past ExperiencesPast simple vs present perfectLife chapters narrativeWrite 5 sentences about last week
3The Retirement Dream -- Plans and GoalsGoing to, will, present continuous for futureVision board discussionRecord your retirement plans
4A Life of Travel -- Describing PlacesAdjective order, comparatives, superlativesTravel photo storytellingDescribe your favorite trip
5Health and Wellness -- Self-Care LanguageModal verbs (should, could, might)Wellness routine role-playRecord daily routine in English
6At the Airport -- Checking In and BoardingPolite requests (Could I...?, Would you mind...?)Airport check-in simulationWatch airport scene from a film
7Hotel Check-in -- Reservations and RequestsWould like to, need to, have toHotel role-play with problemsWrite a hotel complaint email
8Getting Around -- TransportationGiving and following directions, prepositions of placeNavigation challengeDescribe directions to your favorite place
9Eating Out -- Restaurants and Dietary NeedsOrdering language, dietary vocabularyRestaurant role-playRecord ordering a meal
10Shopping Abroad -- Markets and BargainingQuantifiers, prices, too/enoughMarket negotiationDescribe your last shopping experience
11Small Talk -- Conversation with StrangersQuestion forms, tag questionsSpeed-dating style conversationsStart 3 conversations in English this week
12Sharing Opinions -- Agreeing and DisagreeingOpinion frames, hedging languageDebate on travel topicsRecord opinion on a podcast episode
13Telling Stories -- Narrative SkillsPast continuous, time sequencersStorytelling challenge (Canada hospital)Write your Canada hospital story
14Cultural Differences -- Comparing CustomsUsed to, would (past habits)Cross-cultural comparison discussionCompare Brazilian and Australian customs
15Making Friends Abroad -- Building ConnectionsPhrasal verbs for relationshipsSocial event role-playWrite a message to a new friend
16Medical Situations Abroad -- Describing SymptomsPresent perfect continuous, body vocabularyHospital emergency role-playRecord describing a health issue
17Money Matters -- Banking and PaymentsPassive voice (basic), financial vocabularyBank and currency exchange role-playExplain your travel budget
18Technology and Gadgets -- Digital LifeRelative clauses, tech vocabularyTech support conversationDescribe your Oura Ring in English
19Renting and Driving -- Cars and NavigationConditional type 1 (if + present, will)Car rental role-playRecord directions for a road trip
20Midterm Review -- Integration CheckAll structures from lessons 1-19Full travel simulation (airport to hotel)Self-assessment recording
21Discover Australia -- Geography and CulturePassive voice, geographical vocabularyAustralia quiz and discussionResearch 3 Australian cities
22Australian English -- Slang and AccentInformal register, Australian expressionsAussie dialogue comprehensionWatch Australian YouTube video
23The Great Barrier Reef -- Nature and EnvironmentEnvironmental vocabulary, conditionals type 2Eco-tourism debateRecord opinion on nature conservation
24City Life -- Sydney, Melbourne, BeyondRecommendations (You should, I would suggest)City guide presentationPlan a 3-day Sydney itinerary
25Outback Adventures -- ActivitiesGerunds and infinitivesAdventure booking role-playDescribe your dream adventure
26Movies Without Subtitles -- Film DiscussionReview language, adjectives for filmsFilm review and discussionWatch Eat Pray Love scene without subtitles
27Podcasts and Audiobooks -- Listening SkillsReported speech (basic)Podcast summary and discussionListen to Hidden Brain episode
28History in English -- Discussing EventsPast perfect, time clausesHistorical timeline discussionRecord summary of a historical event
29Psychology in English -- Behavior and MindAbstract vocabulary, cause and effectPsychology TED Talk discussionDescribe a personality trait in English
30Technology Trends -- InnovationFuture forms review, predictionsTech debateRecord tech prediction
31Debates and Discussions -- Defending ViewsDiscourse markers, concession languageFormal debateWrite argument for and against
32Emotional Intelligence -- Complex FeelingsVocabulary for emotions, wish + pastEmotional scenario role-playsDescribe a challenging emotion in English
33The Art of Persuasion -- ConvincingPersuasive language, rhetorical questionsPersuasion challengeRecord persuasive pitch
34Problem-Solving Abroad -- Unexpected SituationsThird conditional, regret languageCrisis simulationWrite about a travel problem you solved
35Hosting and Being a Guest -- Social EventsSocial register, invitationsDinner party role-playWrite a dinner invitation email
36Travel Planning -- Australia ItineraryAll future forms, planning languageComplete itinerary presentationFinalize your Australia itinerary
37At Immigration -- Confidence Under PressureQuestion handling, formal registerImmigration interview simulationPractice immigration Q&A
38Your English Portfolio -- Reviewing ProgressSelf-assessment language, reflectionProgress presentationRecord before/after comparison
39Full Immersion Day -- Travel SimulationAll structures integrated90-min immersion: airport to dinnerReflection journal
40Graduation -- Celebrating and Next StepsFuture plans, celebration languageFinal presentation + celebrationLetter to future self in English

Welcome, Pricila!

Onboarding

Bem-vinda ao programa! Antes de cada aula, complete as atividades de Pre-class. Aqui estão 5 frases de emergência que você pode usar a qualquer momento:

1 Could you repeat that, please? Poderia repetir, por favor?
2 I am not sure I understand. Could you explain? Não tenho certeza se entendi. Poderia explicar?
3 Let me think about that for a moment. Deixe-me pensar um momento.
4 To be honest, I have always wanted to... Para ser honesta, eu sempre quis...
5 I have been studying English for many years. Estudo inglês há muitos anos.
Aula 1 -- Pre-class

Reintroducing Myself at B2

Conte a sua historia com vocabulario rico (turning point, milestone, thriving) e fale do que voce vem fazendo com o present perfect continuous (I have been + -ing).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Your Story

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for telling the story of your life and your reinvention.

A career path -- trajetoria profissional
"My career path has taken several unexpected turns."
A turning point -- um ponto de virada
"Retiring became a real turning point for me."
To take stock -- fazer um balanco / refletir
"I have been taking stock of what really matters."
Fulfilling -- gratificante / que da sentido
"Learning English is the most fulfilling thing I do."
To look back on -- relembrar / olhar para tras
"I look back on my career with real pride."
A milestone -- um marco importante
"Opening my own clinic was a huge milestone."
To be passionate about -- ser apaixonado por
"I have always been passionate about travel."
To come a long way -- evoluir muito / progredir bastante
"My English has come a long way this year."
To juggle -- conciliar varias coisas ao mesmo tempo
"I have been juggling new hobbies and travel plans."
Demanding -- exigente / que pede muito esforco
"Dentistry was a demanding but rewarding career."
A fresh perspective -- um novo olhar / perspectiva renovada
"Travel has given me a fresh perspective on life."
To thrive -- prosperar / florescer
"She has been thriving since she retired."
Well-rounded -- completo / com interesses variados
"I want to be a more well-rounded person in this chapter."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

A turning point
To take stock
Fulfilling
To look back on
A milestone
Passionate about
To come a long way
To juggle
Demanding
A fresh perspective
To thrive
Well-rounded

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's reintroduction and answer the questions.

"Let me reintroduce myself. For most of my life I was a dentist -- a demanding but rewarding career path. Recently I reached a real turning point, and I have been taking stock of what truly matters. These days I have been juggling travel, languages and new interests, and honestly, learning English has been the most fulfilling part of all. When I look back on my milestones, I feel proud -- but I have come a long way, and I genuinely feel I am only just getting started."

1. What does Pricila say she has been doing recently?
A Taking stock of what truly matters.
B Going back to full-time dentistry.
C Avoiding any new activities.
2. Which structure does she use for ongoing recent actions?
A Past simple ("I worked").
B Present perfect continuous ("I have been juggling").
C Future ("I will juggle").
3. How does Pricila feel about her journey?
A She regrets her career.
B She is proud and feels she is just getting started.
C She feels her best years are over.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect Continuous

Grammar

Para falar de uma acao que comecou no passado e continua ate agora (muitas vezes com for / since), use have / has + been + verbo-ing. Ela destaca a DURACAO e o esforco recente. / To talk about an action that started in the past and is still going on (often with for / since), use have / has + been + verb-ing. It highlights the duration and recent effort.

Forma / FormEnglishPortugues
AffirmativeI have been learning English for a year.Estudo ingles ha um ano.
Affirmative (he/she)She has been thriving since she retired.Ela vem prosperando desde que se aposentou.
NegativeI have not been wasting my time.Eu nao venho perdendo tempo.
InterrogativeWhat have you been doing lately?O que voce vem fazendo ultimamente?
Common mistakeNOT "I study English since 2000." Say "I have been studying since 2000."Com since / for, use have been + -ing.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I taking stock of my life."
"Retiring was a real turning ."
"My English has come a long ."
"What have you doing lately?"
"Learning English is the most thing I do."
"Travel has given me a fresh on life."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's reintroduction in order.

?These days, she has been juggling travel, languages and new interests.
?Finally, she says she has come a long way and feels she is just getting started.
?First, she explains that dentistry was a long and demanding career path.
?After that, she calls learning English the most fulfilling part of all.
?Then she reaches a turning point and starts taking stock of what matters.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the natural contraction "I've been".

I have been taking stock of my life.
Tenho feito um balanco da minha vida.
Retiring became a real turning point for me.
Me aposentar virou um verdadeiro ponto de virada.
My English has come a long way.
Meu ingles evoluiu muito.
I am still curious and well-rounded.
Continuo curiosa e com interesses variados.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

An old colleague asks what you have been doing since you stepped back from the clinic. What do you say?
A "I have been juggling travel, languages and new hobbies."
B "I will do nothing forever."
C "I did dentistry yesterday."
You want to describe a moment that changed the direction of your life. Which phrase fits?
A "It was a small daily routine."
B "It was a real turning point for me."
C "It was a demanding mistake."
Someone assumes you have stopped growing. How do you correct them politely?
A "Yes, my best years are over."
B "Actually, I have come a long way and I am still thriving."
C "I never learn anything new."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Reintroduce yourself at a B2 level in 5-6 sentences. Mention one milestone you look back on with pride, one turning point in your life, and what you have been doing lately. Use at least three present perfect continuous forms ("I have been..."). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 1

1I have been taking stock of my life.Tenho feito um balanco da minha vida.
2Retiring was a real turning point.Me aposentar foi um verdadeiro ponto de virada.
3My English has come a long way.Meu ingles evoluiu muito.
4I have been juggling travel and new hobbies.Tenho conciliado viagens e novos hobbies.
5I am still thriving and just getting started.Continuo prosperando e so estou comecando.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

My Life in Chapters

Past Experiences & Present Reality: Past simple vs Present perfect
0%
▼

Callback -- Lesson 1 Review

Review

Antes de começar, vamos revisar 4 palavras da Aula 1. Tente lembrar sem olhar!

1. Complete: "Learning English has been a long _____ for me."
A struggle
B journey
C routine
2. Complete: "I want to _____ my dream of being fluent."
A accomplish
B explore
C retire

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Listen — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Chapter -- a period or important part of your life
"My career as a dentist was an important chapter of my life."
Milestone -- an important event or achievement in life
"Graduating from dental school was a major milestone."
Overcome -- to succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty
"I overcame my fear of speaking English at the hospital in Canada."
Memorable -- worth remembering; very special
"My trip to Canada was the most memorable experience of my life."
Decade -- a period of ten years
"I worked as a dentist for over two decades."
Transform -- to change completely in form or character
"That trip to Canada transformed the way I see English."
Reflect -- to think carefully about past experiences
"When I reflect on my career, I feel proud of what I built."
Achievement -- something important you have done successfully
"Running my own dental clinic was my greatest achievement."
Challenge -- something difficult that tests your ability
"Speaking English at the hospital was a real challenge."
Opportunity -- a chance to do something positive
"Retirement is an opportunity to explore the world."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição. Faça um de cada vez.

Chapter
Milestone
Overcome
Memorable
Decade
Transform
Reflect
Achievement
Challenge
Opportunity

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila's life has had many chapters. She graduated from dental school in 1995 — that was a major milestone. She opened her own clinic in Araras and worked there for over two decades. In the year 2000, she traveled to Canada. It was a memorable trip because her daughter got sick and Pricila had to speak English at the hospital. She overcame her fear and communicated with the doctors. That experience transformed how she sees English. She has reflected on that moment many times. Looking back, it was both a challenge and an opportunity. She has achieved a lot in her career, and now retirement is her next chapter.

1. When did Pricila graduate from dental school?
A In 1995 — a specific time in the past (past simple).
B She has graduated recently.
C She graduates every year.
2. Which sentence describes something connected to the present?
A She traveled to Canada in 2000.
B She has reflected on that moment many times.
C Her daughter got sick during the trip.
3. What transformed how Pricila sees English?
A Graduating from dental school.
B The hospital experience in Canada where she had to speak English.
C Opening her own clinic in Araras.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Past Simple = completed actions at a SPECIFIC time (ações completas em um momento ESPECÍFICO)
Present Perfect = experiences connected to NOW, no specific time (experiências conectadas ao AGORA, sem tempo específico)

TenseWhen to UseExample
Past SimpleSpecific time: in 2000, last year, yesterday"She graduated in 1995."
Present PerfectNo specific time: ever, never, already, many times"She has achieved a lot."

Common Mistake:

"I have traveled to Canada in 2000."

"I traveled to Canada in 2000."

Com tempo específico (in 2000), use Past Simple — NUNCA Present Perfect.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"My career as a dentist was an important of my life."
"Graduating from dental school was a major ."
"I my fear of speaking English at the hospital."
"That trip was the most experience of my life."
"That experience the way I see English."
"Retirement is a great to explore the world."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize Pricila's life story in chronological order.

?"She opened her own dental clinic in Araras."
?"Now she is preparing for retirement — her next chapter."
?"She grew up in Araras, São Paulo."
?"She traveled to Canada and overcame her fear of speaking English."
?"She graduated from dental school in 1995."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar, não perfeição.

When I reflect on my career, I feel proud of what I built.
Quando reflito sobre minha carreira, sinto orgulho do que construí.
I overcame my fear of speaking English at the hospital in Canada.
Superei meu medo de falar inglês no hospital no Canadá.
Retirement is an opportunity to explore the world.
A aposentadoria é uma oportunidade de explorar o mundo.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. A friend asks: "When did you go to Canada?" Which answer uses the correct tense?
A "I have gone to Canada in 2000."
B "I went to Canada in 2000. It was a memorable experience."
C "I go to Canada in 2000."
2. Someone asks: "Have you ever overcome a big challenge?" Which response is correct?
A "Yes, I have overcame many challenges."
B "Yes, I have overcome many challenges. The biggest was speaking English at a hospital."
C "Yes, I overcame challenges since 2000."
3. Describe your career using both tenses:
A "I graduated in 1995 and opened my clinic. I have worked as a dentist for over two decades."
B "I have graduated in 1995 and have opened my clinic."
C "I graduate in 1995 and work for two decades."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

Tell the story of your life in three chapters: (1) Your career — when did you start, what milestones did you achieve? (2) Your most memorable travel experience — what happened, what did you overcome? (3) Your present and future — what opportunities are you looking forward to? Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1When I was younger, I used to...Quando eu era mais jovem, eu costumava...
2One of the most memorable moments was when...Um dos momentos mais memoráveis foi quando...
3Looking back, I realize that...Olhando para trás, percebo que...
4That experience changed my life because...Essa experiência mudou minha vida porque...
5I have achieved a lot, but my best chapter is still ahead.Conquistei muito, mas meu melhor capítulo ainda está por vir.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

The Retirement Dream

Plans & Goals: Going to, will, present continuous for future
0%
▼

Callback -- Lesson 2 Review

Review

Vamos revisar 4 palavras da Aula 2.

1. "Graduating was a major _____ in my life."
A chapter
B milestone
C decade
2. "I _____ my fear of speaking English."
A reflected
B transformed
C overcame

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Listen — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Goal -- something you want to achieve in the future
"My goal is to travel to at least five countries after I retire."
Plan -- a detailed idea of what you want to do
"I am planning to visit Australia next year."
Dream -- something you hope will happen in the future
"My dream is to speak English with the same richness I have in Portuguese."
Freedom -- the state of being free; no restrictions
"Retirement means freedom to do what I love."
Purpose -- the reason why you do something; meaning in life
"I need a new purpose now that I am leaving my career."
Adventure -- an exciting and unusual experience
"I want every trip to be a real adventure."
Lifestyle -- the way a person lives their life
"I am going to change my lifestyle completely after retirement."
Priority -- something that is more important than other things
"Health and travel are my top priorities right now."
Bucket List -- a list of things you want to do before you die
"Visiting Australia is at the top of my bucket list."
Embrace -- to accept something willingly and enthusiastically
"I want to embrace this new phase of my life with joy."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Goal
Plan
Dream
Freedom
Purpose
Adventure
Lifestyle
Priority
Bucket List
Embrace

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila is going to retire next year. She has many goals for this new chapter. She is planning to visit Australia — it is at the top of her bucket list. Her biggest dream is to speak English with the same richness she has in Portuguese. She is going to change her lifestyle completely. Freedom and adventure are her new priorities. She thinks retirement will give her a new purpose. She is meeting her travel agent next Tuesday to plan the trip. She believes she will feel more confident after this English program. Pricila is ready to embrace this new phase with joy.

1. Which sentence shows a planned future action (going to)?
A "She is going to retire next year."
B "She thinks retirement will give her a new purpose."
C "Freedom and adventure are her priorities."
2. Which sentence shows a fixed arrangement (present continuous for future)?
A "She is going to change her lifestyle."
B "She is meeting her travel agent next Tuesday."
C "She will feel more confident."
3. Which sentence shows a prediction (will)?
A "She is planning to visit Australia."
B "She is meeting her agent next Tuesday."
C "She believes she will feel more confident after this program."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Going to = planos e intenções decididos antes de falar
Will = previsões, promessas, decisões espontâneas
Present Continuous = compromissos agendados (com data/hora definidos)

FormUseExample
Going to + basePlans decided before speaking"I am going to visit Australia."
Will + basePredictions, promises, spontaneous"I think it will be amazing."
Present ContinuousFixed arrangements (date/time set)"I am meeting my agent on Tuesday."

Common Mistake:

"I will visit Australia next year." (when you already decided)

"I am going to visit Australia next year." (plan already made)

Use "going to" para planos já decididos. "Will" é para decisões do momento ou previsões.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"My is to travel to at least five countries."
"Retirement means to do what I love."
"I want every trip to be a real ."
"Health and travel are my top right now."
"Visiting Australia is at the top of my ."
"I want to this new phase of my life with joy."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize these sentences about Pricila's retirement plans in logical order.

?"I am going to explore Australia and embrace new adventures."
?"My goal is to change my lifestyle and find a new purpose."
?"I believe this will be the best chapter of my life."
?"I am going to retire next year. Freedom is my priority."
?"I am planning to visit the places on my bucket list."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare. O objetivo é praticar.

I am going to change my lifestyle completely after retirement.
Vou mudar meu estilo de vida completamente após a aposentadoria.
My dream is to speak English with the same richness I have in Portuguese.
Meu sonho é falar inglês com a mesma riqueza que tenho em português.
I want to embrace this new phase of my life with joy.
Quero abraçar essa nova fase da minha vida com alegria.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. A friend asks: "What are your plans for retirement?" Which answer uses "going to" correctly?
A "I will travel. I will change my life." (sounds spontaneous, not planned)
B "I am going to travel the world. I am planning to visit Australia first."
C "I travel next year. I change my lifestyle."
2. You just decided something right now at a restaurant. Which is correct?
A "I am going to have the fish." (too planned for a spontaneous decision)
B "I will have the fish, please." (spontaneous decision)
C "I am having the fish." (sounds like a fixed arrangement)
3. You have an appointment confirmed. Which sentence is correct?
A "I am meeting my travel agent next Tuesday at 3 PM." (fixed arrangement)
B "I will meet my travel agent next Tuesday." (not emphasizing it is arranged)
C "I meet my travel agent next Tuesday." (missing future marker)

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

Imagine you are at a dinner party and someone asks: "What are your dreams for the next five years?" Record your answer. Talk about: your goals, your bucket list, what freedom means to you, what kind of lifestyle you want, and what adventures you are planning. Use "going to" for your plans and "will" for your predictions. Include at least 5 words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1I am going to...Eu vou... (plano decidido)
2I am planning to...Estou planejando...
3One day, I hope to...Um dia, espero...
4My dream is to...Meu sonho é...
5The most important thing for me is...A coisa mais importante para mim é...
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

A Life of Travel

Describing Places & Experiences: Adjective order, comparatives, superlatives
0%
▼

Callback -- Lesson 3 Review

Review

Vamos revisar vocabulário da Aula 3.

1. "Visiting Australia is at the top of my _____."
A lifestyle
B bucket list
C priority
2. "I want to _____ this new phase of my life with joy."
A plan
B dream
C embrace

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Listen — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Stunning -- extremely beautiful or impressive
"The view from the mountain was absolutely stunning."
Breathtaking -- so beautiful it takes your breath away
"The sunset over the ocean was breathtaking."
Cozy -- warm, comfortable, and relaxing
"We stayed in a cozy little hotel near the beach."
Crowded -- full of too many people
"The market was very crowded, but the atmosphere was amazing."
Remote -- far away from cities; isolated
"The village was so remote that there was no internet."
Vibrant -- full of energy, color, and life
"The city was vibrant, full of music and color."
Scenic -- having beautiful natural views
"We drove along the most scenic road I have ever seen."
Ancient -- very old; from a long time ago
"We visited ancient temples that were over a thousand years old."
Authentic -- real, genuine, not fake
"The food was authentic and completely different from what we have at home."
Unforgettable -- impossible to forget
"That trip was the most unforgettable experience of my life."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Stunning
Breathtaking
Cozy
Crowded
Remote
Vibrant
Scenic
Ancient
Authentic
Unforgettable

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila has traveled to many stunning places. Her trip to Canada was more memorable than any other trip before. Malta was beautiful, but Canada was more challenging. The hospital experience was the most unforgettable moment of all her travels. She stayed in a cozy little hotel in a remote village. The streets were vibrant and full of life — more vibrant than Araras. She visited ancient buildings that were older than anything in Brazil. The food was authentic and more delicious than she expected. The scenic views were breathtaking — the most beautiful she had ever seen. Even though the market was crowded, the experience was unforgettable. It was the best trip of her life.

1. Which sentence uses a comparative correctly?
A "Canada was more challenginger than Malta."
B "Canada was more challenging than Malta."
C "Canada was the most challenging than Malta."
2. Which sentence uses a superlative correctly?
A "It was the most unforgettable moment of all her travels."
B "It was the more unforgettable moment."
C "It was most unforgettable moment."
3. How do you describe Pricila's hotel?
A "A little remote cozy hotel." (wrong adjective order)
B "A cozy little remote hotel." (opinion + size + location)
C "A hotel cozy little remote."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Comparatives = comparing TWO things (comparando DUAS coisas)
Superlatives = the MOST or LEAST of a group (o MAIS ou MENOS de um grupo)
Adjective Order = opinion > size > age > color > origin > material (ordem dos adjetivos)

TypeRuleExample
Short adj + -er1-2 syllables: add -er / -est"older than" / "the oldest"
Long adj + more3+ syllables: more/most + adj"more beautiful than" / "the most beautiful"
Irregulargood > better > best / bad > worse > worst"better than" / "the best"

Common Mistake:

"Canada was more better than Malta."

"Canada was better than Malta."

NUNCA use "more" com adjetivos que já têm forma comparativa (better, worse, older).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"The view from the mountain was absolutely ."
"We stayed in a little hotel near the beach."
"The city was , full of music and color."
"We visited temples that were over a thousand years old."
"The food was and completely different from what we have at home."
"That trip was the most experience of my life."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize this travel description from arrival to departure.

?"The scenic views along the coast were more breathtaking than any photo."
?"We arrived at a stunning beach town on the first day."
?"It was the most unforgettable trip of my life."
?"We checked into a cozy little hotel near the vibrant market."
?"We ate authentic food and explored ancient ruins."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

The sunset over the ocean was breathtaking.
O pôr do sol sobre o oceano era de tirar o fôlego.
We drove along the most scenic road I have ever seen.
Dirigimos pela estrada mais cênica que já vi.
That trip was the most unforgettable experience of my life.
Aquela viagem foi a experiência mais inesquecível da minha vida.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. Someone asks: "How was your trip to Canada?" Which response uses descriptive adjectives best?
A "It was good. Nice place."
B "It was stunning! The scenery was breathtaking, and we stayed in a cozy hotel in a vibrant neighborhood."
C "Canada is big and cold."
2. Compare two cities using a comparative correctly:
A "Sydney is more bigger than Araras."
B "Sydney is bigger and more vibrant than Araras."
C "Sydney is the most bigger city."
3. Describe the best trip of your life using a superlative:
A "My trip to Canada was the most unforgettable experience I have ever had."
B "My trip to Canada was the more unforgettable experience."
C "My trip to Canada was most unforgettable."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

Describe your favorite trip in detail. Where did you go? What was the place like? Use at least 5 adjectives from this lesson. Compare it to another trip you have taken. What made it the most special? Use comparatives and superlatives.

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1The place was...O lugar era...
2It was more... than I expected.Era mais... do que eu esperava.
3It was the most... place I have ever visited.Foi o lugar mais... que já visitei.
4What I loved most about it was...O que mais amei nele foi...
5I would describe it as...Eu descreveria como...
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

Health and Wellness

The Language of Self-Care: Modal verbs (should, could, might)
0%
▼

Callback -- Lesson 4 Review

Review

Vamos revisar vocabulário da Aula 4.

1. "The sunset over the ocean was _____."
A breathtaking
B crowded
C remote
2. "The food was _____ — real, genuine, not fake."
A scenic
B ancient
C authentic

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Listen — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Wellness -- the state of being healthy and happy
"Wellness is not just about your body, it is about your mind too."
Balance -- having the right amount of different things in your life
"You should find a good balance between work and rest."
Mindful -- paying careful attention to the present moment
"Being mindful helps you reduce stress and enjoy the moment."
Symptom -- a sign that something is wrong with your body
"If you have any symptoms, you should see a doctor."
Prescription -- a written order from a doctor for medicine
"The doctor gave me a prescription for the medicine."
Relief -- the feeling when pain or worry goes away
"I felt relief when the headache finally went away."
Prevent -- to stop something from happening
"Exercise and good food can help prevent many diseases."
Recommend -- to suggest something as good or suitable
"I would recommend walking thirty minutes every day."
Recovery -- the process of getting better after illness
"Recovery after surgery takes time and patience."
Habit -- something you do regularly, often without thinking
"Drinking water is a simple but important habit."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Wellness
Balance
Mindful
Symptom
Prescription
Relief
Prevent
Recommend
Recovery
Habit

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

As a dentist, Pricila knows that wellness is about more than teeth. She should find a better balance between work and rest. She could try being more mindful — paying attention to the present moment instead of worrying about the future. When she had a bad headache last month, she noticed the symptoms early and went to the doctor. The doctor said she should rest more and gave her a prescription. She felt great relief when the pain went away. Her doctor recommended walking thirty minutes every day to prevent stress. Pricila thinks she might start yoga too — it could help her recovery from years of standing all day at the clinic. Drinking water and sleeping well are habits she should keep forever.

1. Which sentence gives strong advice (should)?
A "She should find a better balance between work and rest."
B "She might start yoga."
C "She could try being more mindful."
2. Which sentence shows a possibility, not certainty (might)?
A "She should rest more."
B "She thinks she might start yoga too."
C "Her doctor recommended walking."
3. Which sentence gives a softer suggestion (could)?
A "She should keep these habits forever."
B "She might start yoga."
C "She could try being more mindful."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Should = conselho forte, recomendação (strong advice)
Could = sugestão gentil, possibilidade (gentle suggestion)
Might = possibilidade fraca, talvez (weak possibility)

ModalStrengthExample
ShouldStrong advice (90% sure)"You should see a doctor."
CouldGentle suggestion (50-70%)"You could try yoga."
MightWeak possibility (30-50%)"It might help with stress."

Common Mistake:

"You should to rest more."

"You should rest more."

Modais NUNCA usam "to" depois: should + base form (sem "to").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

" is not just about your body, it is about your mind too."
"You should find a good between work and rest."
"If you have any , you should see a doctor."
"I felt when the headache finally went away."
"Exercise can help many diseases."
"Drinking water is a simple but important ."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize this wellness advice from gentlest to strongest.

?"You should see a doctor about those symptoms." (strong advice)
?"It might help to drink more water." (weak possibility)
?"You could try walking thirty minutes a day." (gentle suggestion)

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

You should find a good balance between work and rest.
Você deveria encontrar um bom equilíbrio entre trabalho e descanso.
I would recommend walking thirty minutes every day.
Eu recomendaria caminhar trinta minutos por dia.
Exercise and good food can help prevent many diseases.
Exercício e boa alimentação podem ajudar a prevenir muitas doenças.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. A friend says: "I have been feeling very stressed." What is the best advice?
A "You must stop working immediately."
B "You should try to find a better balance. You could also try being more mindful."
C "You might want to ignore it."
2. You are at a pharmacy abroad and need medicine. What do you say?
A "Give me medicine for headache."
B "I have a headache. Could you recommend something for relief? I do not have a prescription."
C "I want prescription."
3. Your friend asks about your wellness routine. Which response uses modal verbs correctly?
A "I try to walk every day. I think everyone should have good habits. You could try yoga — it might help with stress."
B "I should to walk every day. You must to try yoga."
C "I walk. Yoga is good."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

A friend is planning a big trip but is worried about getting sick abroad. Give them wellness advice for traveling. What should they do before the trip? What could they pack? What habits might help them stay healthy? Use "should", "could", and "might" in your answer. Include at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1You should...Você deveria... (conselho forte)
2You could try...Você poderia tentar... (sugestão gentil)
3It might help to...Talvez ajude... (possibilidade)
4I would recommend...Eu recomendaria...
5The best thing you could do is...A melhor coisa que você poderia fazer é...
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

At the Airport

Checking In and Boarding: polite requests (Could I...? / Would you mind...?)
0%
▼

Callback -- Lesson 5 Review

Review

Antes de viajar, vamos revisar os modais da Aula 5 (Health & Wellness). Esse mesmo "could" volta hoje, agora como pedido educado.

1. Before a long flight, what is good advice? (modal: should)
A "You should arrive at the airport early."
B "You should to arrive early."
C "You arrive early should."
2. A gentle suggestion for a tired traveler uses... (modal: could/might)
A "You must drink water."
B "You could drink some water. It might help."
C "You drink water now."

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Listen — o som ajuda muito a memória.

Check-in -- the desk where you show your ticket and drop off bags
"I went to the check-in desk first."
Boarding pass -- the card or paper that lets you get on the plane
"Please have your boarding pass ready."
Gate -- the door and waiting area where you board your flight
"My flight is boarding at gate B twelve."
Luggage -- all your bags and suitcases for a trip
"My luggage was a little too heavy."
Carry-on -- a small bag you take onto the plane with you
"My carry-on fits under the seat."
Aisle seat -- a seat next to the walking path, easy to stand up
"I like an aisle seat on long flights."
Window seat -- a seat next to the window, with a view outside
"Could I have a window seat, please?"
Delay -- when a flight leaves later than the planned time
"There is a short delay before boarding."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Check-in
Boarding pass
Gate
Luggage
Carry-on
Aisle seat
Window seat
Delay

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila arrives early at the check-in desk for her flight to Sydney. She smiles and says, "Could I have a window seat, please?" The agent checks her boarding pass and asks how many bags she wants to check. "I would like to check one bag," Pricila answers. "This is my carry-on." Her luggage is a little heavy, so the agent asks, "Would you mind moving a few things?" "Not at all," says Pricila. Then she asks, "Could you tell me which gate it is?" The agent says gate B twelve, but there is a short delay. Pricila stays calm and polite — and that makes everything easier.

1. Which question asks FOR something (for herself)?
A "Could I have a window seat, please?"
B "Would you mind moving a few things?"
C "Could you tell me which gate it is?"
2. Which question asks someone else to DO something?
A "I would like to check one bag."
B "Could you tell me which gate it is?"
C "This is my carry-on."
3. After "Would you mind", which form is correct?
A "Would you mind to move a few things?"
B "Would you mind moving a few things?"
C "Would you mind moved a few things?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Could I...? = pedir algo para mim (more polite than "Can I")
Could you...? = pedir para o outro fazer algo
Would you mind + -ing? = pedido muito educado (resposta: "Not at all")
I would like + to + verb = dizer um desejo com educação

FunctionStructureExample
Ask for meCould I + have/get"Could I have a window seat?"
Ask you to actCould you + verb"Could you check my bag?"
Very politeWould you mind + V-ing"Would you mind waiting?"
State a wishI would like + to + verb"I would like to check one bag."

Common Mistake:

"Would you mind to help me?"

"Would you mind helping me?"

Depois de "Would you mind", o verbo SEMPRE termina em -ing.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each polite request with the correct word.

"Could I have a seat, please?"
"Could you my bag, please?"
"Would you mind me with my luggage?"
"I would to check one bag."
"There is a short before boarding."
"Could you tell me where the is?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Order these from the LEAST polite to the MOST polite request.

?"Can I have a window seat?" (neutral)
?"Give me a window seat." (direct / not polite)
?"Could I have a window seat, please?" (most polite)

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

Could I have a window seat, please?
Eu poderia ter um assento na janela, por favor?
Would you mind helping me with my luggage?
Você se importaria de me ajudar com a minha bagagem?
Excuse me, could you tell me which gate it is?
Com licença, você poderia me dizer qual é o portão?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. Your suitcase is heavy and you need help. What do you say to the agent?
A "Carry my bag now."
B "Would you mind helping me with my luggage?"
C "You help me with bag."
2. You want to choose where you sit on the plane. What is the best request?
A "Could I have an aisle seat, please?"
B "I want aisle seat."
C "Give me aisle."
3. There is a delay and you are not sure about your gate. What do you ask?
A "Where gate?"
B "Tell me gate fast."
C "Could you tell me where the gate is, please?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

Imagine you are checking in for your dream flight to Australia. Tell the agent everything you need, politely: which seat you want, how many bags you are checking, and what you want to know about the gate and the flight. Use "Could I...", "Could you...", "Would you mind...", and "I would like to...". Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1Could I have a window seat, please?Eu poderia ter um assento na janela, por favor?
2Could you check my bag, please?Você poderia despachar a minha mala, por favor?
3Would you mind helping me with my luggage?Você se importaria de me ajudar com a bagagem?
4I would like to check one bag.Eu gostaria de despachar uma mala.
5Excuse me, where is the gate?Com licença, onde fica o portão?
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

At the Hotel

Checking In and Asking for Help: would like to / need to / have to
0%
▼

Welcome back, Pricila!

Onboarding

You landed in Sydney and found your way out of the airport. This week you arrive at your hotel: how to check in, ask for the room you want, and solve problems — with would like to, need to, and have to. Review these emergency phrases before class.

I have a reservation under the name Adamo.
I would like a room with a view, please.
What time do I have to check out?
Is breakfast included?

Callback -- Lesson 6 Review

Review

Você saiu do aeroporto (Aula 6). Vamos revisar os pedidos educados antes de chegar ao hotel.

1. At the airport you asked for a seat. Which is the polite request?
A "Could I have a window seat, please?"
B "Give me window seat."
C "I want window."
2. After "Would you mind", which form is correct?
A "Would you mind to help me?"
B "Would you mind helping me?"
C "Would you mind helped me?"

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo. Toca em Listen.

Reception -- the front desk where you check in and ask for help
"Please call reception for help."
Reservation -- a room you booked in advance
"I have a reservation under the name Adamo."
Key card -- the card that opens your hotel room door
"Here is your key card for room eight."
Check-out -- when you leave the hotel and pay your bill
"What time is check-out?"
Double room -- a room with a bed for two people
"I booked a double room for five nights."
Floor -- a level of the building
"My room is on the eighth floor."
Amenities -- extra services a hotel offers (wifi, pool, gym)
"The hotel has great amenities."
Complaint -- when you tell staff that something is wrong
"I would like to make a complaint."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Reception
Reservation
Key card
Check-out
Double room
Floor
Amenities
Complaint

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila arrives at the hotel reception. "I have a reservation under the name Adamo," she says. "I would like a room with a view, if possible." The receptionist gives her a key card for the eighth floor. "You have to check out by eleven," she explains, "and breakfast is part of the amenities." Later, the air conditioning stops working. Pricila needs to change rooms, so she calls reception: "I would like to make a complaint. I need to change my room, please." The staff fix it quickly. Pricila stays calm and polite — and gets exactly what she needs.

1. Which sentence shows a polite desire?
A "I would like a room with a view."
B "You have to check out by eleven."
C "I need to change my room."
2. Which sentence is a hotel RULE (obligation)?
A "I would like a room with a view."
B "You have to check out by eleven."
C "I would like to make a complaint."
3. Which sentence shows a personal necessity?
A "Breakfast is part of the amenities."
B "I need to change my room."
C "Here is your key card."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

would like (to) = quero / gostaria (educado)
need to = preciso (necessidade pessoal)
have to = tenho que (regra / obrigação)

FunctionStructureExample
Desirewould like + noun / + to + verb"I would like to check in."
Necessityneed to + verb"I need to change my room."
Obligationhave to + verb"You have to check out by 11."

Common Mistake:

"I need change my room."

"I need to change my room."

Sempre use "to + verbo" depois de would like, need e have.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I would a room with a view."
"I need print a document."
"I have to out by eleven."
"I would like to in, please."
"I need to my room."
"Is breakfast ?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Order these check-in steps from FIRST to LAST.

?"I would like a room with a view, please."
?"Good evening, I have a reservation under the name Adamo."
?"Thank you. What time do I have to check out?"

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

I would like a room with a view, please.
Eu gostaria de um quarto com vista, por favor.
I need to change my room. The air conditioning is not working.
Eu preciso trocar de quarto. O ar-condicionado não está funcionando.
What time do I have to check out?
A que horas eu tenho que fazer o check-out?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. The TV in your room does not work. What do you say to reception?
A "TV broken. Fix now."
B "I would like to make a complaint. The TV is not working. Could you help me?"
C "I want other room."
2. You want to know the hotel rule for leaving. What do you ask?
A "What time do I have to check out?"
B "When I go?"
C "I check out now?"
3. You want a quiet room on a high floor. What is the best request?
A "Give me high floor."
B "I would like a quiet room on a high floor, if possible."
C "High floor I want."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

You just arrived at your dream hotel in Sydney. Check in with the receptionist: say you have a reservation, ask for the room and amenities you want, ask about check-out, and report one small problem. Use "I would like to...", "I need to...", and "have to". Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1I have a reservation under the name Adamo.Eu tenho uma reserva no nome Adamo.
2I would like a room with a view, please.Eu gostaria de um quarto com vista, por favor.
3What time do I have to check out?A que horas eu tenho que fazer o check-out?
4My key card is not working. Could you help me?Meu cartão-chave não está funcionando. Você pode me ajudar?
5Is breakfast included?O café da manhã está incluído?
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

Getting Around

Directions & Public Transport: imperatives + prepositions of place
0%
▼

Welcome back, Pricila!

Onboarding

You are settled in your hotel. This week you explore the city: how to ask for directions, understand the answer, and take the metro. Review these emergency phrases before class.

How do I get to the station?
Take the blue line and get off at Central.
Where do I buy a ticket?
Do I have to transfer?

Callback -- Lesson 7 Review

Review

Você está instalada no hotel (Aula 7). Antes de explorar a cidade, vamos revisar.

1. At the hotel, how do you state a desire politely?
A "I would like a room with a view, please."
B "Give me view room."
C "I want view."
2. Which sentence is a rule (obligation)?
A "You have to check out by eleven."
B "I would like breakfast."
C "I need to rest."

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo. Toca em Listen.

Subway -- the underground train (also called the metro)
"Let us take the subway downtown."
Ticket -- the paper or card you buy to travel
"I need to buy a ticket."
Platform -- where you wait for and get on the train
"The train leaves from platform two."
Transfer -- to change from one line or train to another
"You have to transfer at the next station."
Fare -- the price of a trip on public transport
"The fare is four dollars."
Stop -- a place where the bus or train stops
"Get off at the third stop."
Line -- a metro route (the blue line, the red line)
"Take the blue line to the center."
Exit -- the way out of a station or building
"The exit is across from the platform."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Subway
Ticket
Platform
Transfer
Fare
Stop
Line
Exit

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila wants to visit the Opera House. At the subway station she asks an officer the way. "Take the blue line and get off at Circular Quay," he says. "Go straight and the exit is across from the harbour." She buys a ticket from the machine — the fare is four dollars — and finds platform two. She does not have to transfer; the train is direct. Four stops later, she walks out of the station and the Opera House is right there, next to the water.

1. Which sentence GIVES a direction (imperative)?
A "Take the blue line and get off at Circular Quay."
B "The fare is four dollars."
C "She buys a ticket."
2. Which words are prepositions of place?
A "take, get off"
B "across from, next to"
C "ticket, fare"
3. Does Pricila have to transfer?
A Yes, twice.
B No, the train is direct.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Imperatives = verbo primeiro, sem sujeito (Go, Take, Turn, Get off)
Prepositions of place = onde algo fica (next to, across from, between, opposite, behind, on the corner)

UseStructureExample
DirectionVerb first"Take the blue line."
LocationIt is + preposition + place"It is across from the exit."
Ask the wayHow do I get to + place?"How do I get to the museum?"

Common Mistake:

"It is across the station."

"It is across from the station."

Use a preposição completa: across FROM, next TO, in front OF.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"Take the blue line and off at Central."
"The exit is from the platform."
"You have to at the next station."
" do I get to the museum?"
"Go straight and turn at the corner."
"Where do I buy a ?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Order the metro journey steps from FIRST to LAST.

?"Take the blue line to Central."
?"Buy a ticket at the machine."
?"Get off and find the exit."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

How do I get to the Opera House?
Como eu chego até a Ópera?
Take the blue line and get off at Central.
Pegue a linha azul e desça em Central.
The ticket machine is next to the exit.
A máquina de bilhetes fica ao lado da saída.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You are lost and want to reach the museum. What do you ask?
A "Museum where?"
B "Excuse me, how do I get to the museum?"
C "I go museum now?"
2. A tourist asks you the way. How do you give a clear direction?
A "Go straight, turn left, and the station is across from the bank."
B "Straight, left, across the bank."
C "You go to straight."
3. You are not sure if the train is direct. What do you ask?
A "Train direct?"
B "Do I have to transfer, or is it direct?"
C "I transfer where always?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

A friend is visiting your city for the first time. Explain how to get from the airport to the city center by public transport: which line or bus, where to buy a ticket, how many stops, and where to get off. Use imperatives and prepositions of place. Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1How do I get to the station?Como eu chego até a estação?
2Take the blue line and get off at Central.Pegue a linha azul e desça em Central.
3Where do I buy a ticket?Onde eu compro um bilhete?
4Do I have to transfer?Eu preciso fazer baldeação?
5The exit is across from the platform.A saída fica em frente à plataforma.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Eating Out

Restaurants & Dietary Needs: ordering language + some/any
0%
▼

Welcome back, Pricila!

Onboarding

You explored the city by metro and found a great restaurant. This week: how to read a menu, order a full meal, and explain any allergy or diet. Review these emergency phrases before class.

Could we see the menu, please?
I will have the fish, please.
Is there any nut in this dish?
I am allergic to nuts.

Callback -- Lesson 8 Review

Review

Você pegou o metrô até o restaurante (Aula 8). Vamos revisar direções antes de pedir o jantar.

1. How do you ask the way to a place?
A "How do I get to the restaurant?"
B "Restaurant where go?"
C "I get restaurant how?"
2. Which preposition of place is correct?
A "The exit is across the platform."
B "The exit is across from the platform."
C "The exit is across at the platform."

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo. Toca em Listen.

Menu -- the list of food and drinks you can order
"Could we see the menu, please?"
Starter -- the small first dish of a meal (appetizer)
"I will have soup as a starter."
Main course -- the biggest, central dish of a meal
"For my main course, the fish, please."
Dessert -- the sweet dish at the end of a meal
"For dessert, the chocolate cake."
Waiter -- the person who takes your order and serves you
"The waiter brought our food."
Allergy -- when your body reacts badly to a food
"I have a nut allergy."
Vegetarian -- a person who does not eat meat
"Do you have a vegetarian option?"
Bill -- the paper that shows how much you must pay
"Could we have the bill, please?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Menu
Starter
Main course
Dessert
Waiter
Allergy
Vegetarian
Bill

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

The waiter brings the menu. "Could we see the menu, please?" Pricila asks. For her starter, she says, "I will have the soup." For her main course, "I would like the grilled fish." She wants some water, but first she checks: "Is there any nut in the dessert? I am allergic to nuts." Her friend is vegetarian, so Pricila asks if there is a meat-free option. At the end, she says, "Could we have the bill, please?" A polite guest always gets great service.

1. Which sentence orders a dish politely?
A "I will have the soup."
B "Give me soup."
C "Soup now."
2. Why does Pricila use "any" (not "some")?
A Because it is a question.
B Because it is positive.
C There is no rule.
3. How does she explain her allergy?
A "I have allergy of nuts."
B "I am allergic to nuts."
C "I allergic nuts."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Ordering: I will have / I would like + dish (educado, não “I want”)
some / any: some em frases positivas, any em perguntas e negativas
Dietary: I am allergic to... / I cannot eat... / I am vegetarian

UseStructureExample
OrderI will have / I would like"I will have the fish."
some (+)some + noun (positive)"I would like some water."
any (? / −)any + noun (question / negative)"Is there any nut?"
Dietaryallergic to / cannot eat"I am allergic to nuts."

Common Mistake:

"Is there some nuts in this?"

"Is there any nut in this?"

Use "any" em perguntas e negativas; "some" em frases positivas.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I will the fish, please."
"Could we have the , please?"
"Is there nut in this dish?"
"I am allergic nuts."
"Could we have the , please?"
"Do you have a option?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Order these restaurant steps from FIRST to LAST.

?"I will have the soup and the fish."
?"Could we see the menu, please?"
?"Could we have the bill, please?"

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

Could we see the menu, please?
Poderíamos ver o cardápio, por favor?
I will have the soup as a starter, and the fish as a main course.
Vou querer a sopa de entrada e o peixe de prato principal.
Is there any nut in this dish? I am allergic to nuts.
Tem alguma castanha neste prato? Eu sou alérgica a castanhas.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. The waiter asks if you are ready. You want more time. What do you say?
A "No. Wait."
B "Could we have a few more minutes, please?"
C "Me no ready."
2. You cannot eat seafood. How do you tell the waiter?
A "I am allergic to seafood. Is there any in this dish?"
B "No seafood me."
C "I have allergy seafood."
3. You finished and want to pay. What do you say?
A "Money now."
B "I pay where?"
C "Could we have the bill, please?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

You are at a restaurant in Sydney with a friend who is vegetarian and allergic to nuts. Order a full meal for both of you: ask for the menu, choose a starter, main course and dessert, check the ingredients, and ask for the bill. Use "I will have...", "some/any", and "allergic to". Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1Could we see the menu, please?Poderíamos ver o cardápio, por favor?
2I will have the fish, please.Vou querer o peixe, por favor.
3Is there any nut in this dish?Tem alguma castanha neste prato?
4I am allergic to nuts.Eu sou alérgica a castanhas.
5Could we have the bill, please?Poderíamos ter a conta, por favor?
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Shopping Abroad

Markets & Bargaining: quantifiers, prices, too/enough
0%
▼

Welcome back, Pricila!

Onboarding

Last day in Sydney! Before you fly home, you want a souvenir. This week: how to ask prices, say something is too expensive, and bargain for a discount. Review these emergency phrases before class.

How much is this?
That is too expensive.
Can you give me a discount?
Do you take cash?

Callback -- Lesson 9 Review

Review

Você jantou e pagou a conta (Aula 9). Vamos revisar antes de ir ao mercado.

1. How do you order politely at a restaurant?
A "I will have the fish, please."
B "Give me fish."
C "Fish now."
2. Which is correct in a question?
A "Is there any nut in this?"
B "Is there some nuts in this?"
C "Is there a nuts in this?"

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Olha a palavra, a definição e o exemplo. Toca em Listen.

Market -- an open place where many people sell things
"I love shopping at the local market."
Stall -- a small table or stand at a market
"That stall sells beautiful scarves."
Discount -- money taken off the normal price
"Can you give me a discount?"
Cash -- money in coins and notes (not a card)
"Do you take cash?"
Change -- the money you get back when you pay too much
"Here is your change."
Bargain -- to talk with the seller to get a lower price
"At the market, you can bargain."
Size -- how big or small something is
"Do you have a smaller size?"
Souvenir -- a small gift to remember a trip or place
"This scarf is a perfect souvenir."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com sua definição.

Market
Stall
Discount
Cash
Change
Bargain
Size
Souvenir

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Pricila walks through the market and stops at a colorful stall. She sees a scarf and asks, "How much is this?" The seller says forty dollars. "That is too expensive," Pricila replies. "It is not cheap enough for me. Can you give me a discount?" They bargain, and the price drops to thirty-five. Pricila asks for a smaller size and pays with cash. The seller gives her the change. "I will take it," she smiles. "It is a perfect souvenir."

1. How does Pricila ask the price?
A "How much is this?"
B "How much costs?"
C "What price this?"
2. Which sentence uses "too" correctly?
A "That is too expensive."
B "That is expensive too much."
C "That is enough expensive."
3. How does she ask for a lower price?
A "Make discount."
B "Can you give me a discount?"
C "Price down please."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

How much is + singular / How much are + plural (preço)
too + adjetivo = demais (problema): too expensive
adjetivo + enough = suficiente: cheap enough / not cheap enough

UseStructureExample
Ask priceHow much is / are...?"How much is this?"
Too muchtoo + adjective"It is too expensive."
Enoughadjective + enough"It is not cheap enough."
BargainCan you give me a discount?"Can you give me a discount?"

Common Mistake:

"It is enough expensive."

"It is too expensive."

"too" vem ANTES do adjetivo; "enough" vem DEPOIS do adjetivo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"How is this scarf?"
"It is expensive."
"It is not cheap ."
"Can you give me a ?"
"Do you take ?"
"I will it."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Order the bargaining steps from FIRST to LAST.

?"That is too expensive. Can you give me a discount?"
?"How much is this scarf?"
?"I will take it."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça, grave e compare.

How much is this scarf?
Quanto custa este lenço?
That is too expensive. Can you give me a discount?
Isso é caro demais. Você pode me dar um desconto?
Do you have a smaller size? Do you take cash?
Você tem um tamanho menor? Você aceita dinheiro?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. A souvenir costs more than you want to pay. What do you say?
A "Too much money no."
B "That is a bit too expensive. Can you give me a discount?"
C "Price bad."
2. You want to know the price of two hats together. What do you ask?
A "How much are these two hats?"
B "How much is these hats?"
C "How many cost hats?"
3. The price is good and you decide to buy. What do you say?
A "I buy."
B "Great, I will take it. Do you take cash?"
C "Give me this."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês.

You are at a market in Sydney on your last day. You want to buy souvenirs for your family. Choose two or three items, ask the prices, react if they are too expensive, bargain for a discount, ask about sizes, and pay with cash. Use "How much...?", "too / enough", and bargaining language. Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1How much is this?Quanto custa isso?
2That is too expensive.Isso é caro demais.
3Can you give me a discount?Você pode me dar um desconto?
4Do you take cash?Você aceita dinheiro?
5I will take it.Vou levar.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Small Talk -- Conversation with Strangers

Comece conversas leves com desconhecidos durante as viagens: comente o clima, faca perguntas e use tag questions para soar natural.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Small Talk Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you start friendly conversations with strangers.

Stranger -- desconhecido / estranho
"I started a chat with a friendly stranger at the cafe."
Icebreaker -- quebra-gelo
"A comment about the weather is a classic icebreaker."
To chat -- conversar / bater papo
"We chatted for ten minutes while waiting in line."
Local -- morador local / nativo do lugar
"The locals were so friendly and gave us great tips."
Coincidence -- coincidencia
"What a coincidence -- we are both from Brazil!"
In common -- em comum
"We have a lot in common: we both love travel."
Awkward -- constrangedor / sem graca
"The silence felt awkward, so I asked a simple question."
Polite -- educado / cortes
"It is polite to smile and say hello first."
Small world -- como o mundo e pequeno
"You know my cousin? Small world!"
To get along -- se dar bem (com alguem)
"We got along right away and exchanged numbers."
To break the ice -- quebrar o gelo
"A friendly question is the best way to break the ice."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Stranger
Icebreaker
To chat
Local
Coincidence
In common
Awkward
Polite
To get along
To break the ice

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the conversation and answer the questions.

Pricila is waiting for a train in London. A friendly stranger smiles and says, "Beautiful station, isn't it?" That simple comment is a perfect icebreaker. Pricila replies and they begin to chat. "Where are you from?" the man asks. "I'm from Brazil," she says. "You've been to Rio, haven't you?" he laughs. "What a coincidence -- my daughter lives there!" They have a lot in common, and the silence is no longer awkward. It really is a small world.

1. How does the stranger break the ice?
A By commenting on the beautiful station.
B By complaining about the train.
C By asking for money.
2. Which sentence is a tag question?
A "Where are you from?"
B "Beautiful station, isn't it?"
C "I'm from Brazil."
3. Why is it a "small world"?
A The station is very small.
B His daughter lives in Brazil, where Pricila is from.
C They live on the same street.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Questions & Tag Questions

Grammar

Para fazer small talk, voce combina perguntas diretas (Where are you from?) e tag questions -- aquele "ne?" no final da frase. A tag inverte a forma do verbo: frase afirmativa leva tag negativa, e vice-versa. / To make small talk, you mix direct questions with tag questions (the "ne?" at the end). The tag reverses the verb: a positive sentence takes a negative tag, and the other way around.

Tipo / TypeEnglishPortugues
Wh- questionWhere are you from?De onde voce e?
Yes/No questionDo you like the city?Voce gosta da cidade?
Tag (+ frase / - tag)It's a lovely day, isn't it?E um dia lindo, ne?
Tag (- frase / + tag)You aren't from here, are you?Voce nao e daqui, ne?
Tag (past)You enjoyed the trip, didn't you?Voce curtiu a viagem, ne?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"It's a beautiful city, ?"
"You're a tourist here, ?"
" are you from?"
"A friendly question is a great way to break the ."
"What a , we are both from Brazil!"
"We have a lot in ."

Stage 2: Order the Conversation

Order

Listen first, then put the small talk in order.

?Next, Pricila says she is from Brazil and they discover a coincidence.
?Finally, they wish each other a nice trip and say goodbye.
?First, a stranger smiles and comments on the lovely weather.
?After that, they chat about the friendly locals and the beautiful city.
?Then he asks if Pricila is a tourist as well.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the rising and falling melody of tag questions.

Lovely weather today, isn't it?
Que tempo bom hoje, ne?
Where are you from?
De onde voce e?
You're a tourist too, aren't you?
Voce tambem e turista, ne?
It was nice chatting with you.
Foi bom conversar com voce.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You sit next to a stranger on a long flight. How do you break the ice?
A "Hi! Are you traveling for work or for fun?"
B "Give me your phone number."
C Say nothing for ten hours.
A local says, "Beautiful day, isn't it?" What do you reply?
A "I don't know you."
B "Yes, it really is! Are you from here?"
C "Goodbye."
You want to end a friendly chat politely. What do you say?
A "Stop talking now."
B "It was lovely chatting with you. Enjoy your trip!"
C "You are boring."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are waiting in a long line at a museum abroad. Start a small talk conversation with the stranger next to you. Use an icebreaker, ask two questions, and use at least one tag question. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 11

1Lovely weather today, isn't it?Que tempo bom hoje, ne?
2Are you a tourist here too?Voce tambem e turista aqui?
3What a coincidence!Que coincidencia!
4We have a lot in common.Temos muita coisa em comum.
5It was nice chatting with you.Foi bom conversar com voce.
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Sharing Opinions -- Agreeing & Disagreeing

Diga o que voce pensa de forma educada: de opinioes, concorde, discorde com gentileza e justifique suas ideias durante uma conversa de viagem.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Opinion Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you share what you think and react to other people's ideas.

Opinion -- opiniao
"In my opinion, the old town is the best part of the city."
To agree -- concordar
"I totally agree with you about the food here."
To disagree -- discordar
"I have to disagree -- I think the beach is better."
Point of view -- ponto de vista
"I understand your point of view, but I see it differently."
To prefer -- preferir
"I prefer mountains to the beach."
A good point -- um bom argumento
"That's a good point -- I hadn't thought of that."
To be honest -- para ser sincero(a)
"To be honest, I didn't enjoy the museum."
On the other hand -- por outro lado
"It's expensive; on the other hand, the view is amazing."
It depends -- depende
"It depends on the weather and on your mood."
To convince -- convencer
"He convinced me to try the local dish."
To see your point -- entender seu argumento
"I see your point, and you may be right."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Opinion
To agree
To disagree
Point of view
To prefer
A good point
To be honest
On the other hand
It depends
To convince

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the conversation and answer the questions.

On a tour bus, a traveler shares his opinion: "In my opinion, the food is the best part of this trip." Pricila agrees in part: "That's a good point, but I'd say the nature is just as special." The traveler smiles. "I see your point. On the other hand, you can taste the culture at the market." "To be honest," Pricila replies, "I prefer to do both. It depends on the day!" They both laugh -- he almost convinced her to visit the food stalls first.

1. What is the traveler's opinion?
A Food is the best part of the trip.
B Nature is the best part of the trip.
C The tour bus is too slow.
2. How does Pricila disagree politely?
A She says he is completely wrong.
B She says "That's a good point, but..." and adds her view.
C She says nothing at all.
3. What does Pricila finally prefer?
A Only the food.
B Only the nature.
C To do both -- it depends on the day.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Giving & Reacting to Opinions

Grammar

Para dar uma opiniao em ingles, voce abre a frase com expressoes como "In my opinion" ou "I think", e justifica com "because". Para concordar ou discordar com educacao, comece com algo gentil ("That's a good point") antes de adicionar sua propria ideia. / To give an opinion, open with "In my opinion" or "I think" and support it with "because". To agree or disagree politely, start with something kind ("That's a good point") before you add your own view.

Funcao / FunctionEnglishPortugues
Give an opinionIn my opinion, the city is lovely.Na minha opiniao, a cidade e linda.
AgreeI totally agree with you.Concordo totalmente com voce.
Disagree (polite)That's a good point, but I'd say...E um bom argumento, mas eu diria...
Give a reasonI prefer mountains because they are quiet.Prefiro montanhas porque sao calmas.
Stay neutralIt depends; on the other hand...Depende; por outro lado...

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"In my , the old town is the best part."
"That's a good , but I see it differently."
"I mountains because they are quiet."
"I totally with you about the food."
"It's expensive; on the other , the view is amazing."
"To be , I didn't enjoy the museum."

Stage 2: Order the Conversation

Order

Listen first, then put the opinion exchange in order.

?Next, the traveler points out a good reason for visiting markets.
?Finally, they agree to explore a food market together.
?First, the traveler shares his opinion that food is the best part of a trip.
?After that, they realize they both enjoy local experiences.
?Then Pricila partly agrees but adds her own view about nature.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Keep your voice warm when you disagree.

In my opinion, the nature is the best part.
Na minha opiniao, a natureza e a melhor parte.
That's a good point, but I'd say nature is special too.
E um bom argumento, mas eu diria que a natureza tambem e especial.
I see your point, and you may be right.
Entendo seu argumento, e voce pode ter razao.
To be honest, it depends on the day.
Para ser sincera, depende do dia.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A traveler says the museum was boring, but you loved it. How do you disagree politely?
A "You're completely wrong."
B "I see your point, but to be honest, I really enjoyed it."
C Say nothing and look away.
Someone asks if the beach or the mountains are better. You can't decide. What do you say?
A "It depends on the weather, but I prefer the mountains."
B "I don't know anything."
C "Both are the same."
You want to share an opinion and give a reason. Which sentence is best?
A "The food. Good."
B "In my opinion, the food is the best part because you taste the culture."
C "Maybe yes, maybe no."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

A travel friend says, "Big cities are the best place to travel." Share your own opinion. First react politely (agree or disagree), then give your view and one reason. Use at least two opinion expressions. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 12

1In my opinion, ...Na minha opiniao, ...
2That's a good point.E um bom argumento.
3I see your point.Entendo seu argumento.
4To be honest, ...Para ser sincero(a), ...
5It depends.Depende.
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Making Plans & Invitations

Convide pessoas e combine programas: faca convites, sugira horarios e lugares, aceite com calor e recuse com gentileza durante uma viagem.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Plan Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you invite people and arrange to meet.

To invite -- convidar
"I'd like to invite you to dinner tonight."
A plan -- um plano / programa
"Do you have any plans for the weekend?"
Available -- disponivel / livre
"Are you available on Saturday morning?"
To join -- juntar-se / participar
"Come and join us for a walk along the harbor."
A suggestion -- uma sugestao
"Can I make a suggestion? Let's meet a bit later."
To meet up -- encontrar-se
"Let's meet up at the lobby at ten."
To work for -- servir / ser bom para
"Does Friday work for you, or is Saturday better?"
Sounds good -- parece otimo
"Lunch by the water? Sounds good to me!"
To turn down -- recusar (educadamente)
"I had to turn down the trip because I was tired."
To reschedule -- remarcar
"Can we reschedule the tour for the afternoon?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To invite
A plan
Available
To join
A suggestion
To meet up
To work for
Sounds good
To turn down
To reschedule

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the conversation and answer the questions.

Mark sends Pricila a message: "Would you like to join us tomorrow? We are going to the old harbor." Pricila is happy: "I'd love to! What time shall we meet up?" Mark replies, "How about meeting at the lobby at ten? Does that work for you?" Pricila thinks for a moment. "Could we reschedule for half past ten? I'd like a slow breakfast." Mark agrees: "Of course. Why don't we have lunch by the water after the walk?" "Sounds good -- count me in!" Pricila says.

1. How does Mark invite Pricila?
A He says "Would you like to join us tomorrow?"
B He says "You must come with us."
C He says nothing at all.
2. Why does Pricila ask to reschedule?
A She does not want to go.
B She would like a slow breakfast first.
C She is not available at all.
3. How does Pricila accept the plan in the end?
A "I'm afraid I can't make it."
